title: Commentary on Investments: As of December 31, 2026
content:
Portfolio Composition
Total investments stood at $1,128,520 (in thousands), representing a 2.0% increase versus the previous year end and a 0.5% quarter-over-quarter change as of March 31, 2026. Available-for-sale debt securities comprised approximately 97.6% of the portfolio at $1,102,120 (in thousands), providing flexibility to sell before maturity if liquidity needs arise.

Minor Holding Category and Change Drivers
Time deposits totaled $2,285 (in thousands) as of March 31, 2026. The quarter-over-quarter decline in total investments was driven by a -23.2% reduction in time deposits during the period; other components remained stable or increased slightly.

Maturity Structure and Liquidity Implications
The maturity distribution chart shows assets spread across multiple horizons: <=1 year, >1-3 years, >3-5 years, >5-10 years, and >10 years. This well-distributed structure may support near-term liquidity flexibility while maintaining longer-duration positions for yield enhancement.

Closing Integration and Monitoring Focus
The portfolio’s composition—dominated by available-for-sale debt securities with a diversified maturity profile—positions the credit union to balance predictable income generation with adaptable access to funds under varying demand conditions. Management will continue monitoring shifts in time deposit balances, given their recent contraction and potential impact on overall investment liquidity.
